text: This intraoperative photograph shows the sympathetic trunk and the pre- and post-ganglionic fibers.
The high-definition three-dimensional camera of the robotic surgical system is crucial for the visualization of these very fine structures.
We're looking at a left-sided procedure.
The procedure begins by marking the second, third, and fourth ribs in order to have a clear view of the anatomy.
The pleura overlying the sympathetic chain, which is just under the left grasper in the video, is dissected and peeled away, creating a window which allows us to see all of the structures clearly.
It's crucial that the dissection be done in a bloodless fashion as any staining with blood will obliterate the anatomy.
As we continue the dissection, the sympathetic chain begins to emerge.
The concept of the surgery is to divide the preganglionic and postganglionic fibers to and from the chain and also the connecting fibers that are next to the chain and underneath the chain.
At the end of the operation, the chain will be elevated and will be free of all connecting structures.
Here, as you can see, the pleura is being dissected.
The chain is emerging.
High definition three-dimensional visualization and the excellent instrument mobility of the robot and maneuverability allows for this very fine dissection and identification of all these structures.
The dissection is carried to the top of the second rib.
You can see the rib has been identified with the cautery mark.
It's important not to go above that
because going above will increase the risk of Horner's syndrome as a result of damaging the fibers that go up to the stellate ganglion.
The resection preserves the intercostal vessels and only divides the connecting fibers to the chain.
It's important to understand that there will be a preganglionic fiber for each segment and a postganglionic fiber and also the communicating fibers which will be
parallel to the chain and behind the chain.
Here, for the second rib area, we are dissecting the chain and we will be lifting the chain, as you can see.
